subject.title: Synthetic self-dogfood measurement example for Evidence Sprint

collectionBoundaries.approvalAuthority: human maintainers and repository branch protection remain the approval authority
collectionBoundaries.reportOnlyReason: The fixture fixes the measurement vocabulary before dogfooding and pilots run; it does not approve work or create a policy-gate block condition.

sourceArtifacts.description: Synthetic stand-in for the Issue intent used to start a dogfood or pilot observation.
Plan artifact that defines task and evidence expectations for the observation.
First runnable verification evidence used for the timing metric.
Claim-to-evidence mapping used to count evidence coverage, missing evidence, and unresolved claims.
Representative review-thread closure signal; live paths remain private and are replaced by fixture or redacted artifact references in public reports.
Completion audit evidence that distinguishes required checks from advisory, skipped, and stale workflow findings.
Human-readable template that future self-dogfood and pilot issues can copy.

time_to_first_runnable_verification_minu.purpose: Measure onboarding and execution friction from accepted Issue or requirement intent to the first runnable verification artifact.
time_to_first_runnable_verification_minu.calculationMethod: Minutes between the observation's accepted requirement timestamp and the first passing runnable verification timestamp.
time_to_first_runnable_verification_minu.interpretation: Lower values may indicate a smoother reference flow only when task size, queueing, and branch-policy assumptions are comparable.
time_to_first_runnable_verification_minu.limitation: This fixture uses synthetic timestamps; it supports instrumentation readiness, not an adoption-speed claim.

evidence_coverage.purpose: Show how many required evidence items are present and reviewable before public or merge-readiness claims are made.
evidence_coverage.calculationMethod: Required evidence items marked present divided by all required evidence items in the observation checklist.
evidence_coverage.interpretation: Higher coverage means more of the workflow can be inspected through artifacts rather than prose alone.
evidence_coverage.limitation: Coverage does not prove evidence sufficiency, freshness, or correctness; reviewers still need to inspect the artifacts.

missing_evidence_count.purpose: Keep unsupported claims visible before they are copied into a case study, release note, or announcement.
missing_evidence_count.calculationMethod: Count required evidence items or PR-relevant claims whose source artifact is absent or marked not_collected.
missing_evidence_count.interpretation: Non-zero values identify follow-up work or publication limitations; they are not automatically blocking unless policy says so.
missing_evidence_count.limitation: A count can change when the claim set changes, so denominators and claim boundaries must be recorded with the observation.

unresolved_claim_count.purpose: Measure whether PR-relevant claims have a final disposition before completion audit or publication packaging.
unresolved_claim_count.calculationMethod: Count PR-relevant claims without evidence, waiver, explicit deferral, or non-applicability disposition at the final snapshot.
unresolved_claim_count.interpretation: Zero unresolved claims supports traceable closeout for the scoped claim set.
unresolved_claim_count.limitation: Zero does not prove the implementation is correct; it only means the recorded claim set has dispositions.

review_rework_count.purpose: Capture how many review-feedback cycles required code, fixture, or documentation changes after the first PR draft.
review_rework_count.calculationMethod: Count actionable review-feedback clusters that resulted in follow-up commits, excluding informational comments and already-outdated duplicates.
review_rework_count.interpretation: Higher counts can indicate useful risk discovery or unclear first-pass evidence; classify the cause before treating it as negative.
review_rework_count.limitation: Review style varies across reviewers and tools; do not compare teams or vendors from this count alone.

deterministic_replay_pass.purpose: Record whether the same local fixture and commands can reproduce the report-only evidence surface.
deterministic_replay_pass.calculationMethod: Pass when the documented local fixture/render/validation commands complete without drift or schema failure.
deterministic_replay_pass.interpretation: A passing replay supports reproducibility of the evidence package.
deterministic_replay_pass.limitation: Local fixture replay is not a live hosted-agent or external-repository pilot.

manual_intervention_count.purpose: Track human actions needed for approval, redaction, environment repair, or publication-boundary decisions.
manual_intervention_count.calculationMethod: Count intervention records in the observation log using the fixed taxonomy from the protocol.
manual_intervention_count.interpretation: Non-zero values identify product friction or governance steps that must remain explicit.
manual_intervention_count.limitation: Some interventions are required controls, not defects; classify the reason before comparing observations.

audit_discrepancy_count.purpose: Count differences between required-check completion wording and workflow/advisory findings discovered during completion audit.
audit_discrepancy_count.calculationMethod: Count completion-audit notes where required checks, advisory findings, skipped runs, stale runs, or local verification were previously conflated.
audit_discrepancy_count.interpretation: Non-zero values show where completion comments need more precise wording or follow-up tracking.
audit_discrepancy_count.limitation: Discrepancy counts depend on how thoroughly advisory surfaces were inspected.

allowedClaims: The repository has a report-only fixture and template for collecting Evidence Sprint metrics without live external APIs.
The metric vocabulary evaluates ae-framework workflow effectiveness rather than raw agent or vendor quality.
forbiddenClaims: Do not claim faster review, faster implementation, safer code, or external adoption from this synthetic fixture.
Do not rank Codex, Copilot, Claude Code, or any other producer from these metrics.
Do not treat report-only metric values as approval, merge authority, or a new policy-gate block condition.
claimBoundaries.notes: One dogfood or pilot run can support instrumentation-readiness and traceability statements, but not controlled improvement claims.

limitations: Synthetic fixture only; no live external repository PR was measured.
No live external agent API or live GitHub API was called to create the fixture.
Timing values are deterministic example values and must not be reused as public performance claims.
Publication approval evidence is intentionally missing so the fixture demonstrates the limitation path.
summary: Report-only Evidence Sprint measurement fixture: first runnable verification 34 minutes, evidence coverage 5/6, missing evidence count 1, unresolved claim count 0, review rework count 2, deterministic replay pass true, manual intervention count 1, audit discrepancy count 1.
